history.content:
<thought>
The agent is facing forward, and the local map shows a wall line ahead with goal visible to the right. Forward movement is blocked by a wall ('#'), so moving forward is not an option. The goal is two cells to the right and one cell ahead. Turning right will face the agent toward the goal, and the path to the right appears clear of walls or lava near the starting position. I will TURN_RIGHT to start moving toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
After turning right, I now face north. The goal 'G' is visible to the left of my position, and there's a floor cell '.' directly forward which is safe to move into. I should move forward to progress toward the goal area and adjust my position before turning left to approach the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
After moving forward, I see that the goal 'G' is now to the left of the agent. The path forward is blocked by a wall, so moving forward is not possible. To reach the goal, I need to turn left and then move forward toward the goal cell. I will first TURN_LEFT to face the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am now facing west, and there is a floor cell '.' right in front of me, which is safe to move into. The goal 'G' is visible nearby, two cells ahead and to the right. I should move forward first to get closer to the goal path.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am facing west again. In front of me is a lava cell 'L', which is deadly, so I cannot move forward. To reach the goal 'G' which is to the right, I should turn right to face north, then later move towards the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
Now I am facing north and the cell in front of me is a goal 'G', which means I can move forward to reach the goal safely.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
